title: Development of Algorithmic Game Theory
description: A timeline showing the key milestones in the development of Algorithmic Game Theory, culminating in the First Indian Edition of a major textbook, highlighting its roots in foundational work and its evolution into a modern field.
elements: Foundational Work (1950s-60s): Nash Equilibrium & Game Theory
Rise of Computer Science (1970s-80s): Complexity Theory & Algorithms
Convergence (1990s-2000s): Birth of Algorithmic Game Theory
Textbook Standardization (2007): Nisan et al.'s 'Algorithmic Game Theory'
Global Adoption & Education (2010s+): First Indian Edition
relationships: Chronological progression showing how earlier elements influenced later ones, with the First Indian Edition representing the field's maturity and widespread academic adoption.
